failure_modes.text: Reflection can become a hiding place that postpones commitment, trade-offs, or the next concrete move.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When the Pause Becomes a Hiding Place: The model fails when reflection is used to postpone commitment, avoid trade-offs, or keep options abstract rather than to improve the next concrete move.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a reflection deadline: agree in advance on when the pause must produce a concrete next action, trade-off, or commitment.

failure_modes.text: Teams can boil the ocean when stepping back turns into over-comprehensiveness instead of sharpening what matters.
failure_modes.source_quote: An anti-pattern in research where one loses the big picture and gathers an overwhelming amount of data just for the sake of comprehensiveness.
failure_modes.mitigation: Scope the step-back: define what question the pause is meant to answer, and stop widening the lens once that question is resolved.

failure_modes.text: Perfectionism can turn the pause into analysis paralysis rather than a better-sequenced action path.
failure_modes.source_quote: For those prone to perfectionism, stepping back can lead to over-analyzing everything to achieve perfection, which stymies important action.
failure_modes.mitigation: Convert insight to action: after stepping back, require the first output to be a sequenced action plan, not more analysis.

premortem_questions.text: What is the core point, and why does it matter?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What’s your main (core) point? Why does it matter?

premortem_questions.text: Is it the most important thing you could be doing right now?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is it the most important thing you could be doing right now?

heuristics.text: Use the pause to recover the big picture and governing purpose before spending more effort on local detail.
heuristics.source_quote: The step back forces the articulation of the main goal or core message, ensuring every subsequent action is aligned with a single purpose.

heuristics.text: Switch from intuitive reaction to deliberate framing before committing to a plan in high-stakes or ambiguous situations.
heuristics.source_quote: By stepping back, one consciously engages the rational mind ... rather than defaulting to habitual, bias-prone shortcuts.

heuristics.text: Reduce the problem into logical pieces only after the needed output is clear enough that the extra analysis will serve a real decision.
heuristics.source_quote: work plans are crystal clear about what output is needed to solve the problem before modeling or analysis begins.

heuristics.text: Deliberately disconnect from stress-inducing noise long enough for the pause to restore framing, not just absorb more agitation.

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on pause-for-clarity, anti-detail-fog discipline, and forcing reflection to improve the next move rather than delay it.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor reflection sprawl because the raw source is stronger on deliberate pause, big-picture recovery, and anti-hiding-place discipline.
open_questions: The third heuristic compresses several process-integrity passages so the model stays operational rather than philosophical.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on systems thinking, metacognitive questioning, and first-principles-like reset pressure for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is strongest on confirmation-bias, status-quo-bias, and commitment-bias-style default continuation.
structured_tensions: The source includes an explicit structured tension block around commitment-bias, status-quo-bias, and confirmation-bias that cleanly maps into the current relation layer vocabulary.
